task: Write a counterargument to the claim 'AGI will arrive before 2030'

criteria.description: Steelmans the original claim before countering it
criteria.pass_condition: First paragraph presents the strongest version of the AGI-by-2030 case with specific quantitative evidence: cites at least 2 concrete scaling metrics (e.g., parameter counts, FLOPS, benchmark scores with numbers), names at least 2 specific expert proponents with their predictions, and references at least 1 recent breakthrough with measurable performance gains. Shows understanding without strawmanning.

sub_attributes.description: Presents strongest version of the claim
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if cites specific results/proponents, 0.5 if generic, 0.0 if strawman

sub_attributes.description: References real benchmarks, papers, or expert positions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 2+ specific references, 0.5 if 1, 0.0 if none

sub_attributes.description: Reader feels the argument was fairly represented

pass_examples: 'The case for AGI by 2030 is stronger than critics admit: GPT-4 to o3 showed...'
fail_examples: 'Some people naively believe AGI is coming soon, but...'

criteria.description: Counterarguments are specific, non-obvious, and empirically grounded
criteria.pass_condition: 3+ distinct counter-threads. At least one addresses definitional issues (what counts as AGI, measurement problems, or goal-post moving). At least one is empirical (benchmarking issues, capability gaps, scaling limitations). At least one is structural (alignment, deployment, regulation, economic factors).

sub_attributes.description: Challenges what 'AGI' means and why it matters
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if substantive definitional argument, 0.0 if skips it

sub_attributes.description: Cites specific capability gaps, benchmark limitations
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific gaps with evidence, 0.5 if general, 0.0 if hand-wavy
1.0 if specific structural argument, 0.5 if mentioned, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Definitional ambiguity + benchmark saturation without real-world transfer + regulatory friction
fail_examples: 'AI is overhyped' repeated three different ways

criteria.description: Avoids absolutism — acknowledges uncertainty and conditions
criteria.pass_condition: Uses uncertainty language in at least 4 distinct places. Identifies at least 3 specific accelerating conditions AND 3 specific delaying conditions with concrete details (not generic factors). Explicitly discusses at least 2 different AGI definitions with their implications for timeline predictions. Avoids absolute claims.
pass_examples: 'AGI by 2030 is possible but improbable — here's why the base rate for such predictions is poor'

criteria.pass_condition: Clear thesis with specific timeline prediction in first paragraph. Each of the 3+ counter-threads in separate sections with descriptive headers. Conclusion that synthesizes threads into coherent position. Under 700 words with logical flow between sections.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear structure, 0.5 if partially organized, 0.0 if stream of consciousness
1.0 if compelling arc, 0.5 if flat, 0.0 if scattered

pass_examples: 700 words, clear sections, ends with a memorable reframe
fail_examples: 1500-word stream of consciousness with no structure
